channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Takeaways from the AP's investigation into Osprey safety issues
item.description: After being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight service members in Japan, the V-22 Osprey — a complicated aircraft that flies fast like a plane but converts to land like a helicopter — is back in the air. But there are still questions as to whether it should be.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, KEVIN VINEYS and AARON KESSLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane debate at state capitol heats up as temperatures cool down
item.description: Cooler temperatures mean hurricane season is almost behind us, but the debate in the state capital over storm recovery is just heating up.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photo-shoot fail: Biden, Trudeau miss traditional photo with world leaders at G20
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — President Joe Biden inadvertently was a missing man in the traditional photo of world leaders at the final Group of 20 summit of his presidency Monday, a photo-shoot fail that U.S. officials attributed to timing.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau says Canada 'could have acted quicker' on reining in immigration
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said the federal government in Canada "could have acted quicker" to reign in the massive influx of migrants entering the country following the coronavirus pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin Republican Eric Hovde concedes defeat to Democrat Tammy Baldwin in US Senate race
item.description: Wisconsin Republican U.S. Senate candidate Eric Hovde has conceded defeat in the race narrowly won by Democratic Sen. Tammy Baldwin, saying he will not pursue a recount.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Accused of violating worker rights, SpaceX and Amazon go after labor board
item.description: SpaceX and Amazon are asking the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals to find the National Labor Relations Board unconstitutional. The federal agency is tasked with enforcing workers' right to organize.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Just before the election, a poll from Selzer sent shockwaves nationwide after it found Vice President Harris leading President-elect Trump in Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk is hiring for DOGE. Here's the type of people he wants to recruit
item.description: "This will send shockwaves through the system, and anyone involved in government waste, which is a lot of people," Musk said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Ron Johnson says his 'bias would be to vote' for Trump's picks
item.description: Sen. Ron Johnson, R-Wis., said he doesn't "particularly like being the human resource department of administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Library of Congress says an adversary hacked some emails
item.description: The library said that an adversary accessed email communications during the period from January to September.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic AGs rush to form line of defense against Trump
item.description: Democratic attorneys general across the country are readying their legal defenses against the incoming Trump administration, preparing to pounce on potential violations and even take the president-elect to court if he implements controversial policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump allies remain in legal peril despite his election victory
item.description: President-elect Trump’s White House victory set his legal cases on a downward spiral, but his allies who have faced prosecution or other legal trouble are not yet in the clear.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Some in the U.S. farm industry are alarmed by Trump's embrace of RFK Jr. and tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump won landslide support in much of farm country, but his embrace of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his plan for a tariff fight with China alarms many farmers and agriculture experts.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Biden and Xi take a first step to limit AI and nuclear decisions at their last meeting
item.description: The leaders agreed that humans should control decisions to use nuclear weapons — not AI. And they talked about the importance of stability during the transition to a new U.S. administration.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump names Karoline Leavitt as White House press secretary
item.description: Leavitt, 27, previously served in the Trump White House as assistant press secretary under Kayleigh McEnany, now a leading host on Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump-DeSantis complicated relationship could impact Senate pick: political expert
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis has a big decision to make if Sen. Marco Rubio is confirmed as the next Secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Detroit Lions player charged over Capitol riot
item.description: A former Detroit Lions linebacker was arrested and charged with allegedly assaulting law enforcement and other offenses over the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump picks his lawyer, Todd Blanche, as deputy attorney general
item.description: Blanche, a former federal prosecutor, represented Trump in several of his legal cases, including in his landmark federal conviction in New York.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Speaker Mike Johnson: Republicans 'ready to deliver' on Trump's agenda
item.description: Congress returns to a changed Washington as President-elect Donald Trump’s hard-right agenda is quickly taking shape, buoyed by eager Republican allies eyeing a full sweep of power on Capitol Hill while Democrats are sorting out what went wrong.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, WGN 9
